title: Autonomy Kernel Dispatch Packet V1
description: Emitted by the autonomy kernel (AUT-2) when it decides to dispatch a T2 or T3 lane, either for real (t3_live/t2t3_live modes) or as a shadow-mode record. This is the kernel's OWN artifact — distinct from docs/05_operations/schemas/execution-packet-v1.md, which is the pre-existing Codex CLI task packet format. A dispatch packet, once accepted, causes the kernel to invoke the same ops:lane-start / ops:dispatch primitives a human-invoked /dispatch already uses; it does not bypass them.

packet_id.description: UUID or ULID, unique per packet. Never reused across cycles, even for the same issue_id.

tier.description: T1 is not a representable value. A T1 dispatch attempt fails schema validation at emission time, before any mechanical gate runs -- see THREAT_MODEL.md #5. This is a compile-time/validate-time guarantee, not a runtime branch that could be skipped.

executor.description: Which executor the kernel is dispatching to, resolved by the same three-brain routing rules a human orchestrator would use. Must match lane_manifest_v1.schema.json's created_by/executor vocabulary.

mode_at_dispatch.description: The kernel mode in effect at the moment this packet was generated. halted is not a valid value here -- a packet is never generated while halted (STATE_MACHINE.md 3.2).

expires_at.description: Packets are single-cycle artifacts. A packet not acted on by expires_at is discarded, not carried into the next cycle -- the next cycle re-evaluates from current truth and generates a fresh packet if the candidate is still eligible.

file_scope_lock.description: Identical semantics to lane_manifest_v1.schema.json's file_scope_lock. Computed the same way ops:scope-suggest already computes it for a human-invoked lane-start.

passed.description: Must be true for the packet to be actionable. A packet with passed: false is a shadow-mode-only record of a refusal (THREAT_MODEL.md #3) -- it is never dispatched, in any mode.

checked_against.description: Pinned reference so the check's provenance is auditable -- this must always cite the sensitive-path matrix, never a kernel-local copy of it.

matched_paths.description: Non-empty only when passed is false -- the specific sensitive paths that caused refusal.

dispatch_reason.description: Free-text but required -- human-readable justification (e.g. 'top candidate per ops:lane-maximizer, no concurrency/scope conflicts, tier=T3, sensitive-path check passed'). Non-authoritative narrative; the mechanical fields above are what gate the action, this is for audit-log readability.

idempotency_key.description: Deterministic function of (issue_id, action_type, cycle_id) -- e.g. 'UTV2-1234:lane-start:cycle-2026-07-23T18:00Z'. Used on crash/restart to detect whether this exact action was already durably recorded before re-attempting -- see CRASH_RESTART_SEMANTICS.md 3.

checked_at.description: Must be re-checked immediately before this packet is acted on, not just at cycle start -- see KILL_SWITCH_CONTRACT.md 2.

halted.description: A packet with halted: true is a contradiction (the kernel must not have generated a live packet while halted) and fails validation.

dry_run.description: true when mode_at_dispatch is shadow, or when the packet represents a refused/would-not-dispatch record. When true, no mutating call (ops:lane-start, PR open, merge, Linear write) may follow from this packet under any circumstance.

concurrency_snapshot_ref.description: Optional pointer (path or artifact ID) to the ops:execution-state / ops:merge-risk / ops:lane-maximizer output this packet's decision was based on, for post-hoc audit.
